[Project README: /home/ishanp/Documents/GitHub/igs-mcp/README.md]
# IGS MCP Server

**Intelligence Gathering System** — Local-only MCP server for real-time news monitoring and academic research.

[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/igs-mcp-server)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/igs-mcp-server)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/ishan-parihar/igs-mcp)](https://github.com/ishan-parihar/igs-mcp/blob/main/LICENSE)

---

## 🎯 What It Does

IGS is an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that provides AI assistants with:

1. **News Monitoring** — Fetch from 223 curated RSS/HTTP sources (Reuters, BBC, Indian Express, etc.)
2. **Reddit Search** — Search any subreddit with time/sort filters
3. **Academic Research** — Search arXiv + Semantic Scholar papers with PDF extraction
4. **Historical Queries** — Access news from specific date ranges via Google News

All processing happens **locally on your machine**. No data is sent to external servers except the news sources themselves.

---

## 🚀 Quick Start

### 1. Install & Configure (Recommended)

```bash
git clone https://github.com/ishan-parihar/igs-mcp.git
cd igs-mcp
./setup.sh
```

The setup script checks prerequisites (Node.js v20+), installs dependencies, builds, bootstraps config files, and prints your Claude Desktop config at the end — ready to copy-paste.

### Manual Install

```bash
git clone https://github.com/ishan-parihar/igs-mcp.git
cd igs-mcp
npm install
npm run build
```

### 2. Configure Claude Desktop

Edit `claude_desktop_config.json`:

**macOS:** `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`  
**Windows:** `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json`  
**Linux:** `~/.config/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "igs": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["/absolute/path/to/igs-mcp/dist/server.js"]
    }
  }
}
```

### 3. Restart Claude Desktop

Completely quit and relaunch Claude Desktop.

---

## 📚 Documentation

| Document | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [INSTALL.md](INSTALL.md) | Complete installation guide |
| [AUDIT_REPORT.md](AUDIT_REPORT.md) | Installation audit & verification |
| `docs/plans/` | Architecture & design documents |

---

## 🛠️ Available Tools

### News Monitoring

| Tool | Description | Example |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `news.fetch` | Fetch from curated sources | `news.fetch(pools=['GLOBAL_BREAKING'], keywords=['AI'], limit=10)` |
| `news.testSource` | Test single source | `news.testSource(id='reuters')` |

### Research Tools

| Tool | Description | Example |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `reddit.search` | Search Reddit | `reddit.search(query='technology', subreddits=['news'], limit=10)` |
| `research.search` | Search papers | `research.search(query='transformer', sources=['arxiv'], limit=10)` |
| `research.paper` | Paper details + PDF | `research.paper(paperId='arxiv:2401.12345', extractPDF=true)` |

### Source Management

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `sources.list` | List all sources |
| `sources.upsert` | Add/update source |
| `sources.delete` | Remove source |
| `sources.countries` | Countries with source counts |
| `sources.cities` | Cities with source counts |
| `sources.domains` | Topic domains |

### Pool Management

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `pools.list` | List all pools |
| `pools.upsert` | Create/update pool |
| `pools.delete` | Delete pool |

---

## 📊 Pre-configured Content

### News Pools (14)

| Pool | Description | Sources |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `GLOBAL_BREAKING` | Fast breaking news | Reuters, BBC, FT, Al Jazeera |
| `GLOBAL_GEOECON` | Geopolitics & geoeconomics | Foreign Affairs, RAND, Brookings |
| `GLOBAL_LAW_REG` | Law & regulation | OFAC, Lawfare, SCOTUSblog |
| `GLOBAL_TECH_CYBER` | Tech & cyber | MIT Tech Review, Ars Technica, CISA |
| `GLOBAL_ENV_HEALTH` | Environment & health | WHO, Mongabay, Nature |
| `GLOBAL_CULT_SOC` | Culture & society | Aeon, UnHerd, Noema |
| `INDIA_NATIONAL_BASE` | India national | The Hindu, Indian Express, Economic Times |
| `INDIA_WATCHDOG` | India investigative | Scroll.in, The Wire, Article 14 |
| `INDIA_FACTCHECK_DATA` | India fact-check | Alt News, BOOM Live, IndiaSpend |
| `INDIA_BUSINESS_REG` | India business | Moneycontrol, NSE, RBI, SEBI |
| `INDIA_REGION` | India regional | IE South/East/Northeast |
| `INDIA_CITIES` | India cities | Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru feeds |
| `GLOBAL_COUNTRIES` | Country feeds | Guardian/France24 country feeds |
| `GLOBAL_CITIES` | Global cities | London, NYC, Singapore |

### Source Inventory

- **Total Sources:** 223
- **Countries Covered:** 45
- **Cities Covered:** 23
- **Topic Domains:** 23 (geopolitics, finance, tech, science, etc.)

---

## 🔧 Configuration

### User Config Directory

IGS stores configuration in:

- **Linux/macOS:** `~/.config/igs-mcp/`
- **Windows:** `%APPDATA%\igs-mcp\`
- **Custom:** Set `IGS_CONFIG_DIR` environment variable

### Files

```
~/.config/igs-mcp/
├── pools.yml      # Pool definitions
├── sources.yml    # News sources (223 configured)
└── settings.yml   # HTTP/cache settings
```

### Add Custom Source

Edit `~/.config/igs-mcp/sources.yml`:

```yaml
- id: my_tech_blog
  name: My Tech Blog
  type: rss
  url: https://example.com/feed.xml
  parser: rss
  pools: [GLOBAL_TECH_CYBER]
  countries: [US]
  domains: [tech]
  is_active: true
```

No restart required — changes load automatically.

---

## 🏗️ Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│           MCP Client (Claude)               │
└─────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                  │ stdio
┌─────────────────▼───────────────────────────┐
│         IGS MCP Server (Node.js)            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Tools: 11 MCP tools                        │
│  • news.fetch      • research.search        │
│  • reddit.search   • research.paper         │
│  • sources.*       • pools.*                │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Parsers: 9 parsers                         │
│  • RSS/Atom        • Generic HTML           │
│  • Semantic Scholar• PDF extraction         │
│  • OFAC, WHO, USSF (custom HTML)            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  APIs:                                      │
│  • Reddit Search   • arXiv OAI-PMH          │
│  • Semantic Scholar• Google News proxy      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## ⚡ Performance

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Cold start | ~2 seconds |
| news.fetch (9 sources) | ~3 seconds |
| news.fetch (223 sources) | ~25 seconds |
| Cache hit ratio | ~90% (warm) |
| Memory usage | 50-150 MB |

---

## 🔒 Security

- ✅ **Local-only** — Runs on your machine
- ✅ **No telemetry** — No data collection
- ✅ **HTTPS only** — All external requests encrypted
- ✅ **No API keys required** — For basic functionality
- ✅ **User-controlled config** — You own your data

```

## 🐛 Troubleshooting

### Server won't start

```bash
# Check Node version (must be v20+)
node --version

# Rebuild
rm -rf dist/
npm run build
```

### Config not loading

```bash
# Check user config directory
ls -la ~/.config/igs-mcp/

# Force bootstrap
rm -rf ~/.config/igs-mcp/
npm start
```

### Cache issues

```bash
# Clear all caches
rm -rf ~/.config/igs-mcp/cache/
rm -rf ./cache/
```

### Rate limiting

- **Semantic Scholar:** 100 requests/day (free tier)
- **Reddit:** ~60 requests/minute
- **arXiv:** 3000 requests/day

Tool includes automatic retry with backoff.

1: #!/usr/bin/env bun
2: /**
3:  * validate-sources.ts — Pre-validation script for RSS/JSON feeds and other sources.
4:  *
5:  * Tests each source URL for:
6:  *   1. HTTP 200 status
7:  *   2. Content-Type header inspection
8:  *   3. Parsing simulation (where possible)
9:  *
10:  * Usage:
11:  *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser>
12:  *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser> [--url <url2> --parser <parser2> ...]
13:  *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --file <path-to-yaml>
14:  *
15:  * YAML file format (matching sources.yml):
16:  *   sources:
17:  *     - url: https://example.com/feed
18:  *       parser: rss
19:  *       name: optional label
20:  *
21:  * Or a flat list:
22:  *   - url: https://example.com/feed
23:  *     parser: rss
24:  *
25:  * Exit code: 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail
26:  */
27: 
28: import RSSParser from 'rss-parser';
29: import { request } from 'undici';
30: import yaml from 'js-yaml';
31: import fs from 'node:fs/promises';
32: 
33: const rssParser = new RSSParser({ timeout: 15000 });
34: 
35: // ── Types ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36: 
37: interface SourceEntry {
38:   url: string;
39:   parser: string;
40:   name?: string;
41: }
42: 
43: interface ValidationResult {
44:   url: string;
45:   parser: string;
46:   status: 'ok' | 'fail' | 'partial';
47:   items_count: number;
48:   error?: string;
49:   content_type?: string;
50:   feed_type?: string;
51:   sample_title?: string;
52: }
53: 
54: interface Summary {
55:   total: number;
56:   passed: number;
57:   failed: number;
58:   partial: number;
59: }
60: 
61: interface Report {
62:   summary: Summary;
63:   results: ValidationResult[];
64: }
65: 
66: // ── CLI argument parsing ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
67: 
68: function parseArgs(): { entries: SourceEntry[]; filePath: string | null } {
69:   const args = process.argv.slice(2);
70:   const entries: SourceEntry[] = [];
71:   let filePath: string | null = null;
72: 
73:   const fileIndex = args.indexOf('--file');
74:   if (fileIndex !== -1 && fileIndex + 1 < args.length) {
75:     filePath = args[fileIndex + 1];
76:   }
77: 
78:   for (let i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {
79:     if (args[i] === '--url' && i + 1 < args.length) {
80:       const url = args[i + 1];
81:       if (!url.startsWith('http://') && !url.startsWith('https://')) {
82:         console.error(`Invalid URL: ${url}`);
83:         process.exit(1);
84:       }
85:       let parser = 'rss';
86:       for (let j = i + 2; j < args.length; j++) {
87:         if (args[j] === '--url' || args[j] === '--file') break;
88:         if (args[j] === '--parser' && j + 1 < args.length) {
89:           parser = args[j + 1];
90:           break;
91:         }
92:       }
93:       entries.push({ url, parser });
94:     }
95:   }
96: 
97:   return { entries, filePath };
98: }
99: 
100: async function loadSourcesFromYaml(filePath: string): Promise<SourceEntry[]> {
101:   const raw = await fs.readFile(filePath, 'utf-8');
102:   const data = yaml.load(raw) as any;
103: 
104:   if (!data) throw new Error(`Empty or invalid YAML file: ${filePath}`);
105: 
106:   const list: any[] = data.sources ?? (Array.isArray(data) ? data : null);
107:   if (!list) throw new Error(`YAML file must have a "sources" key or be a flat list`);
108: 
109:   return list.map((entry: any, idx: number) => {
110:     if (!entry.url) throw new Error(`Entry ${idx} is missing "url"`);
111:     if (!entry.parser) throw new Error(`Entry ${idx} is missing "parser"`);
112:     return { url: entry.url, parser: entry.parser, name: entry.name };
113:   });
114: }
115: 
116: // ── Feed type detection helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────
117: 
118: function detectFeedType(body: string, contentType: string): string {
119:   const trimmed = body.trim();
120:   if (trimmed.startsWith('<?xml') || trimmed.startsWith('<rss') || trimmed.startsWith('<feed')) {
121:     if (/<rss[\s>/]/i.test(trimmed)) return 'rss';
122:     if (/<feed[\s>/]/i.test(trimmed)) return 'atom';
123:     if (contentType.includes('xml')) return 'xml';
124:     return 'xml';
125:   }
126:   if (trimmed.startsWith('{') || trimmed.startsWith('[')) {
127:     try {
128:       const parsed = JSON.parse(trimmed);
129:       if (parsed?.version && parsed?.items) return 'json_feed';
130:       if (parsed?.items) return 'json_feed_like';
131:       return 'json';
132:     } catch {
133:       return 'json_like';
134:     }
135:   }
136:   if (trimmed.startsWith('%PDF')) return 'pdf';
137:   return 'html';
138: }
139: 
140: // ── Validation per parser type ───────────────────────────────────────────────
141: 
142: async function validateSource(entry: SourceEntry): Promise<ValidationResult> {
143:   const { url, parser } = entry;
144:   const result: ValidationResult = {
145:     url,
146:     parser,
147:     status: 'fail',
148:     items_count: 0,
149:   };
150: 
151:   let body: string;
152:   let statusCode: number;
153:   let rawContentType: string;
154: 
155:   try {
156:     const controller = new AbortController();
157:     const timeout = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), 15000);
158: 
159:     const resp = await request(url, {
160:       method: 'GET',
161:       headers: {
162:         'User-Agent': 'IGS-Validator/1.0 (+https://github.com/ishan-parihar/igs-mcp)',
163:         Accept: 'application/rss+xml, application/json, text/html, application/xml, application/pdf, */*',
164:       },
165:       signal: controller.signal,
166:     });
167: 
168:     clearTimeout(timeout);
169: 
170:     statusCode = resp.statusCode;
171:     rawContentType = (resp.headers['content-type'] as string) || '';
172:     result.content_type = rawContentType;
173: 
174:     if (statusCode !== 200) {
175:       result.status = 'fail';
176:       result.error = `HTTP ${statusCode}`;
177:       return result;
178:     }
179: 
180:     const chunks: Buffer[] = [];
181:     for await (const chunk of resp.body) {
182:       chunks.push(Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? chunk : Buffer.from(chunk));
183:     }
184:     body = Buffer.concat(chunks).toString('utf-8');
185: 
186:     if (body.length === 0) {
187:       result.status = 'fail';
188:       result.error = 'Empty response body';
189:       return result;
190:     }
191:   } catch (err: any) {
192:     result.status = 'fail';
193:     if (err.name === 'AbortError') {
194:       result.error = 'timeout (15s)';
195:     } else if (err.code === 'ENOTFOUND') {
196:       result.error = `DNS resolution failed: ${err.message}`;
197:     } else if (err.code === 'ECONNREFUSED') {
198:       result.error = `Connection refused: ${err.message}`;
199:     } else if (err.code === 'ECONNRESET') {
200:       result.error = `Connection reset: ${err.message}`;
201:     } else if (err.code === 'UND_ERR_HEADERS_TIMEOUT') {
202:       result.error = 'Headers timeout';
203:     } else if (err.type === 'aborted' || err.message?.includes('abort')) {
204:       result.error = 'timeout (15s)';
205:     } else {
206:       result.error = err.message?.slice(0, 200) || String(err);
207:     }
208:     return result;
209:   }
210: 
211:   result.feed_type = detectFeedType(body, rawContentType);
212: 
213:   try {
214:     await simulateParse(parser, body, result);
215:   } catch (parseErr: any) {
216:     result.status = 'fail';
217:     result.error = `Parse error: ${parseErr.message?.slice(0, 200) || String(parseErr)}`;
218:   }
219: 
220:   return result;
221: }
222: 
223: async function simulateParse(
224:   parser: string,
225:   body: string,
226:   result: ValidationResult,
227: ): Promise<void> {
228:   switch (parser) {
229:     case 'rss': {
230:       let feed;
231:       try {
232:         feed = await rssParser.parseString(body);
233:       } catch {
234:         // rss-parser may also handle Atom; if it fails, try as raw XML
235:         feed = { items: [] };
236:       }
237:       const items = feed.items || [];
238:       result.items_count = items.length;
239: 
240:       if (items.length > 0) {
241:         result.status = 'ok';
242:         result.sample_title = items[0]?.title?.slice(0, 120);
243:       } else {
244:         // Check if body at least looks like valid RSS/XML
245:         if (body.includes('<item>') || body.includes('<entry>') || body.includes('<rss') || body.includes('<feed')) {
246:           result.status = 'partial';
247:           result.error = 'Feed parsed but 0 items';
248:         } else {
249:           result.status = 'fail';
250:           result.error = 'Content does not appear to be RSS/Atom XML';
251:         }
252:       }
253:       break;
254:     }
255: 
256:     case 'json_feed': {
257:       let data: any;
258:       try {
259:         data = JSON.parse(body);
260:       } catch {
261:         result.status = 'fail';
262:         result.error = 'Invalid JSON';
263:         return;
264:       }
265: 
266:       const items = Array.isArray(data?.items) ? data.items
267:         : Array.isArray(data) ? data
268:         : Array.isArray(data?.value) ? data.value
269:         : [];
270: 
271:       result.items_count = items.length;
272: 
273:       const hasVersion = typeof data?.version === 'string' || data?.version !== undefined;
274:       const hasTitle = typeof data?.title === 'string';
275: 
276:       if (items.length > 0) {
277:         result.status = 'ok';
278:         result.sample_title = items[0]?.title || items[0]?.url || '(no title)';
279:         result.sample_title = String(result.sample_title).slice(0, 120);
280:       } else if (hasVersion || hasTitle) {
281:         result.status = 'partial';
282:         result.error = 'JSON feed structure recognized but 0 items';
283:       } else if (Array.isArray(data) && data.length > 0) {
284:         // Could be an array of items (some APIs return this)
285:         result.status = 'ok';
286:         result.items_count = data.length;
287:         result.sample_title = String(data[0]?.title || data[0]?.url || '').slice(0, 120);
288:       } else {
289:         result.status = 'fail';
290:         result.error = 'JSON does not match feed structure (missing version/items)';
291:       }
292:       break;
293:     }
294: 
295:     case 'generic_html':
296:     case 'article_content': {
297:       const stripped = body.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '').trim();
298:       if (stripped.length > 0) {
299:         result.status = 'partial';
300:         result.items_count = 0;
301:         result.feed_type = result.feed_type || 'html';
302:       } else {
303:         result.status = 'fail';
304:         result.error = 'Empty or whitespace-only HTML body';
305:       }
306:       break;
307:     }
308: 
309:     case 'sitemap': {
310:       const trimmed = body.trim();
311:       if (trimmed.includes('<urlset') || trimmed.includes('<sitemapindex')) {
312:         const locMatches = body.match(/<loc>/g);
313:         result.items_count = locMatches ? locMatches.length : 0;
314:         result.status = result.items_count > 0 ? 'ok' : 'partial';
315:         if (result.items_count === 0) result.error = 'Sitemap parsed but 0 URLs found';
316:       } else if (trimmed.startsWith('<?xml') || trimmed.startsWith('<')) {
317:         result.status = 'partial';
318:         result.items_count = 0;
319:         result.error = 'XML content but not a recognized sitemap format';
320:       } else {
321:         result.status = 'fail';
322:         result.error = 'Not a sitemap XML';
323:       }
324:       break;
325:     }
326: 
327:     case 'ofac':
328:     case 'ussf_cfc':
329:     case 'newslaundry': {
330:       const stripped = body.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '').trim();
331:       if (stripped.length > 100) {
332:         result.status = 'partial';
333:         result.items_count = 0;
334:       } else {
335:         result.status = 'fail';
336:         result.error = `Body too short for ${parser} parser (${stripped.length} chars text)`;
337:       }
338:       break;
339:     }
340: 
341:     case 'who_dons':
342:     case 'semantic_scholar': {
343:       try {
344:         const data = JSON.parse(body);
345:         result.status = 'partial';
346:         result.items_count = Array.isArray(data) ? data.length
347:           : Array.isArray(data?.items) ? data.items.length
348:           : Array.isArray(data?.results) ? data.results.length
349:           : 0;
350:         if (result.items_count === 0) result.error = 'JSON parsed but 0 items found in expected fields';
351:       } catch {
352:         result.status = 'fail';
353:         result.error = 'Invalid JSON for parser type';
354:       }
355:       break;
356:     }
357: 
358:     case 'pdf-extractor':
359:     case 'pdf-to-markdown': {
360:       if (body.startsWith('%PDF') || body.charCodeAt(0) === 0x25) {
361:         result.status = 'partial';
362:         result.items_count = 0;
363:         result.feed_type = 'pdf';
364:       } else {
365:         result.status = 'fail';
366:         result.error = 'Not a PDF file (missing %PDF header)';
367:       }
368:       break;
369:     }
370: 
371:     default: {
372:       result.status = 'partial';
373:       result.items_count = 0;
374:       result.error = `Unknown parser type "${parser}" — only connectivity verified`;
375:       break;
376:     }
377:   }
378: }
379: 
380: // ── Report ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
381: 
382: function printReport(results: ValidationResult[]): void {
383:   const summary: Summary = { total: 0, passed: 0, failed: 0, partial: 0 };
384: 
385:   for (const r of results) {
386:     summary.total++;
387:     if (r.status === 'ok') summary.passed++;
388:     else if (r.status === 'fail') summary.failed++;
389:     else if (r.status === 'partial') summary.partial++;
390:   }
391: 
392:   const report: Report = { summary, results };
393:   console.log(JSON.stringify(report, null, 2));
394: }
395: 
396: // ── Main ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
397: 
398: async function main() {
399:   const { entries: cliEntries, filePath } = parseArgs();
400:   let allEntries: SourceEntry[] = [...cliEntries];
401: 
402:   if (filePath) {
403:     try {
404:       const fileEntries = await loadSourcesFromYaml(filePath);
405:       allEntries = allEntries.concat(fileEntries);
406:     } catch (err: any) {
407:       console.error(`Error loading YAML file "${filePath}": ${err.message}`);
408:       process.exit(1);
409:     }
410:   }
411: 
412:   if (allEntries.length === 0) {
413:     console.error('No sources to validate. Provide --url/--parser pairs or --file.');
414:     console.error('');
415:     console.error('Usage:');
416:     console.error('  bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser>');
417:     console.error('  bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --file <path-to-yaml>');
418:     process.exit(1);
419:   }
420: 
421:   const results: ValidationResult[] = [];
422: 
423:   for (let i = 0; i < allEntries.length; i++) {
424:     const entry = allEntries[i];
425:     const label = entry.name || entry.url;
426:     process.stderr.write(`[${i + 1}/${allEntries.length}] ${label}... `);
427: 
428:     const result = await validateSource(entry);
429:     results.push(result);
430: 
431:     const icons: Record<string, string> = { ok: '✓', fail: '✗', partial: '~' };
432:     process.stderr.write(`${icons[result.status] || '?'} ${result.status}`);
433:     if (result.items_count > 0) process.stderr.write(` (${result.items_count} items)`);
434:     if (result.error) process.stderr.write(` — ${result.error.slice(0, 80)}`);
435:     process.stderr.write('\n');
436:   }
437: 
438:   printReport(results);
439: 
440:   const anyFailed = results.some(r => r.status === 'fail');
441:   process.exit(anyFailed ? 1 : 0);
442: }
443: 
444: main().catch(err => {
445:   console.error('Fatal error:', err);
446:   process.exit(1);
447: });

(End of file - total 447 lines)
</content>

2: import path from 'node:path';

5: import { PoolsFileSchema, SourcesFileSchema, SettingsSchema, type PoolsFile, type SourcesFile, type Settings } from './schemas.js';
6: 
7: // Resolve user config directory with precedence:
8: // 1) env IGS_CONFIG_DIR
9: // 2) $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/igs-mcp or ~/.config/igs-mcp
10: // Defaults directory (read-only): <repo>/config
11: const DEFAULT_DIR = path.resolve(process.cwd(), 'config');
12: 
13: function resolveUserConfigDir(): string {
14:   const envDir = process.env.IGS_CONFIG_DIR;
15:   if (envDir && envDir.trim().length > 0) return path.resolve(envDir);
16:   const xdg = process.env.XDG_CONFIG_HOME || path.join(os.homedir(), '.config');
17:   return path.join(xdg, 'igs-mcp');
18: }
19: 
20: const USER_CFG_DIR = resolveUserConfigDir();
21: 
22: async function fileExists(p: string): Promise<boolean> {
23:   try { await fs.access(p); return true; } catch { return false; }
24: }
25: 
26: async function ensureBootstrapped() {
27:   // If user config files are missing, copy from DEFAULT_DIR
28:   const files = ['pools.yml', 'sources.yml', 'settings.yml', 'countries.yml'];
29:   await fs.mkdir(USER_CFG_DIR, { recursive: true });
30:   for (const f of files) {
31:     const target = path.join(USER_CFG_DIR, f);
32:     if (!(await fileExists(target))) {
33:       const src = path.join(DEFAULT_DIR, f);
34:       if (await fileExists(src)) {
35:         const raw = await fs.readFile(src);
36:         await fs.writeFile(target, raw);
37:       }
38:     }
39:   }
40: }
41: 
42: async function readYaml<T>(file: string): Promise<T> {
43:   const raw = await fs.readFile(file, 'utf8');
44:   const doc = yaml.load(raw);
45:   return doc as T;
46: }
47: 
48: async function writeYaml(file: string, obj: unknown) {
49:   const txt = yaml.dump(obj, { noRefs: true, lineWidth: 120 });
50:   await fs.mkdir(path.dirname(file), { recursive: true });
51:   await fs.writeFile(file, txt, 'utf8');
52: }
53: 
54: async function mergeMissingDefaultSources() {
55:   const userFile = path.join(USER_CFG_DIR, 'sources.yml');
56:   const defaultFile = path.join(DEFAULT_DIR, 'sources.yml');
57:   if (!(await fileExists(userFile)) || !(await fileExists(defaultFile))) return;
58: 
59:   const [userDoc, defaultDoc] = await Promise.all([
60:     readYaml<SourcesFile>(userFile),
61:     readYaml<SourcesFile>(defaultFile),
62:   ]);
63: 
64:   const userIds = new Set((userDoc.sources || []).map(s => s.id));
65:   const merged = [...(userDoc.sources || [])];
66:   let changed = false;
67: 
68:   for (const src of defaultDoc.sources || []) {
69:     if (!userIds.has(src.id)) {
70:       merged.push(src);
71:       changed = true;
72:     }
73:   }
74: 
75:   if (changed) {
76:     await writeYaml(userFile, { sources: merged });
77:   }
78: }

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # IGS Source Expansion — Learnings
2: 
3: ## Task: Add `platform`, `tier`, `rate_limit` to Source type/schema
4: 
5: ### Files Modified
6: - `src/types/news.ts` — Source type updated
7: - `src/config/schemas.ts` — SourceSchema updated
8: 
9: ### Changes Made
10: 
11: #### Source type changes:
12: - `type` expanded: `'rss' | 'http'` → `'rss' | 'http' | 'social_media'`
13: - `sourceCategory` expanded: `'news' | 'community' | 'research'` → `'news' | 'community' | 'research' | 'social'`
14: - New field: `platform?: 'reddit' | 'twitter' | 'web'`
15: - New field: `tier?: 1 | 2 | 3`
16: - New field: `rate_limit?: { interval_seconds: number; batch_size?: number }`
17: 
18: #### SourceSchema changes:
19: - `type` enum expanded similarly with z.enum
20: - `platform`: `z.enum(['reddit', 'twitter', 'web']).optional()`
21: - `tier`: `z.union([z.literal(1), z.literal(2), z.literal(3)]).optional()`
22: - `rate_limit`: `z.object({ interval_seconds: z.number().int().positive(), batch_size: z.number().int().positive().optional() }).optional()`
23: - `sourceCategory` enum expanded + kept `optional().default('news')`
24: 
25: ### Key Decisions
26: - All new fields are OPTIONAL — backward compatible with existing sources.yml
27: - `platform` defaults to undefined (backward compat implied as 'web' at usage time)
28: - `tier` defaults to undefined (implied Tier 2 at usage time)
29: - `rate_limit` entirely optional — no rate limiting unless explicitly configured
30: - Placed new fields between `is_active` and `sourceCategory` in both type and schema
31: 
32: ### Build Result
33: - `bun run build` passes with zero errors
34: 
35: ---
36: 
37: ## Task: Add 4 new pools to pools.yml + split GLOBAL_ENV_HEALTH
38: 
39: ### Files Modified
40: - `config/pools.yml` — pool definitions updated
41: 
42: ### Changes Made
43: - **Deactivated** `GLOBAL_ENV_HEALTH` (set `is_active: false`) — not removed
44: - **Added 4 new pools** at end of file:
45:   - `GLOBAL_HEALTH` — Pandemics, disease outbreaks, health policy, biosecurity
46:   - `GLOBAL_ENVIRONMENT` — Climate change, biodiversity, energy transition
47:   - `GLOBAL_SCIENCE` — Scientific research, space exploration, discovery
48:   - `GLOBAL_DEFENSE_SECURITY` — Military, defense, aerospace, arms control
49: - `GLOBAL_CULT_SOC` left untouched (already existed, already active)
50: 
51: ### Key Decisions
52: - Deactivated rather than deleted `GLOBAL_ENV_HEALTH` for audit trail
53: - Section header comments follow existing YAML style
54: - YAML validated with Python `yaml.safe_load()`
55: 
56: ---
57: 
58: ## Task: Create centralized rate limiter at `src/utils/rate-limiter.ts`
59: 
60: ### Files Created
61: - `src/utils/rate-limiter.ts` — New file
62: 
63: ### Changes Made
64: 
65: #### RateLimiter class (exported):
66: - `setLimit(key, config)` — Register rate limit config
67: - `check(key)` — Non-mutating check if request is allowed (prunes + checks count)
68: - `wait(key, timeoutMs?)` — Polls every 100ms until slot available or timeout (30s default), records timestamp on success, throws `RateLimitError` on timeout
69: - `remaining(key)` — Remaining slots in current window, `Infinity` if no config
70: - `timeUntilNext(key)` — MS until next slot, 0 if room available or no config
71: - `reset(key)` — Clears timestamps and config for key
72: 
73: #### Named exports:
74: - `RateLimitConfig` interface
75: - `RateLimitError` class (extends Error, has `key` and `retryAfterMs` fields)
76: - `REDDIT_PLATFORM_LIMIT` — 60 req / 60s
77: - `TWITTER_PLATFORM_LIMIT` — 450 req / 15min
78: - `DEFAULT_SOURCE_INTERVAL_MS` — 300000 (5 min)
79: - `sourceRateLimitKey(sourceId)` — Returns `source:${sourceId}`
80: - `platformRateLimitKey(platform)` — Returns `platform:${platform}`
81: 
82: ### Implementation Details
83: - Sliding window: array of timestamps per key, pruned via binary search (`lowerBound`)
84: - No external dependencies — pure in-memory Map storage
85: - `lowerBound` helper function for efficient O(log n) pruning
86: - Default timeout for `wait()` is 30s, polling interval 100ms
87: - JS single-threaded — no locks needed for concurrency
88: 
89: ### Key Decisions
90: - `check()` does NOT record requests (read-only), `wait()` records after acquiring slot
91: - Config stored as spread copy (`{ ...config }`) to prevent external mutation
92: - No limit configured = pass-through (returns true / Infinity / 0)
93: - Single file with no imports from other packages for zero-dependency isolation
94: 
95: ### Build Result
96: - `bun run build` passes with zero errors
97: - Zero LSP diagnostics on new file
98: 
99: ---
100: 
101: ## Task: Create source validation script at `scripts/validate-sources.ts`
102: 
103: ### Files Created
104: - `scripts/validate-sources.ts` — New standalone validation script
105: 
106: ### Usage
107: ```
108: bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser>
109: bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --file <path-to-yaml>
110: ```
111: 
112: ### CLI Interface
113: - `--url <url> --parser <parser>` — Single source pair; can be repeated for multiple sources
114: - `--file <path>` — YAML file with sources (supports both `sources:` key wrapper and flat list format)
115: - Exit code 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail
116: 
117: ### Validation logic per parser type:
118: - **rss**: HTTP fetch → `rss-parser.parseString()` → items > 0 = ok, 0 = partial, parse failure = fail
119: - **json_feed**: HTTP fetch → JSON.parse → check `version` + `items` fields
120: - **generic_html / article_content**: HTTP 200 + non-empty stripped body → partial
121: - **sitemap**: Check for `<urlset>` or `<sitemapindex>` XML structure → count `<loc>` entries
122: - **ofac / ussf_cfc / newslaundry**: Custom HTML → non-empty body > 100 chars stripped → partial
123: - **who_dons / semantic_scholar**: Custom JSON → verify parseable, count items/results → partial
124: - **pdf-extractor / pdf-to-markdown**: Check for `%PDF` magic bytes → partial
125: 
126: ### Output format:
127: ```json
128: {
129:   "summary": { "total": 3, "passed": 2, "failed": 0, "partial": 1 },
130:   "results": [
131:     { "url": "...", "parser": "rss", "status": "ok", "items_count": 40, "content_type": "...", "feed_type": "rss", "sample_title": "..." }
132:   ]
133: }
134: ```
135: 
136: ### Key Decisions
137: - Standalone script — no imports from `src/` to keep it portable and testable
138: - Uses existing deps: `rss-parser`, `undici`, `js-yaml`
139: - Unknown parser types get `partial` status with connectivity verification only
140: - Progress output on stderr, JSON report on stdout (pipeline-friendly)
141: - Feed type auto-detected from body content (rss/atom/json/html/pdf)
142: - Network errors mapped to readable messages (DNS, timeout, connection refused, connection reset)
143: 
144: ### Verification
145: - Tested with BBC RSS (40 items ✓), Daring Fireball Atom (48 items ✓), example.com HTML (~ partial), httpstat.us/404 (✗)
146: 
147: ---
148: 
149: ## Task: Create tier validator utility at `src/utils/tier-validator.ts`
150: 
151: ### Files Created
152: - `src/utils/tier-validator.ts` — Pure utility module
153: 
154: ### Exports
155: - `Tier` type (`1 | 2 | 3`)
156: - `TierConfig` interface — label, description, cacheTtlMultiplier, isAuthoritative
157: - `TIER_CONFIG` const map — `Record<Tier, TierConfig>`
158: - `validateTier(value)` — Accepts numbers and strings `'1'`/`'2'`/`'3'`, returns `Tier | undefined`
159: - `getTierLabel(tier)` — Returns label or `'Unknown Tier'` for undefined
160: - `isSourceReliable(tier?)` — Only Tier 1 is authoritative
161: - `getTierCacheMultiplier(tier?)` — Tier 1 = 2x, Tier 2 = 1x, Tier 3 = 0.5x, undefined → 1.0
162: 
163: ### Key Decisions
164: - Zero dependencies — no imports from `src/types/` or any other module
165: - `validateTier` uses a `Set<Tier>` for O(1) validation of accepted values
166: - All functions return sensible defaults for undefined rather than throwing
167: - No console.log, no TODO/FIXME comments
168: - Section divider comments and JSDoc on public exports kept as necessary API documentation
169: 
170: ### Build Result
171: - `bun run build` passes with zero errors
172: 
173: ## Wave 1, Task 6 — Schema validation enhancements
174: 
175: - Added `.superRefine()` to existing `SourceSchema` (no existing schema fields changed)
176: - Three validation rules added:
177:   1. `type === 'social_media'` → `platform` must be set
178:   2. `tier` must be 1, 2, or 3 if provided (defense-in-depth for the union type)
179:   3. `rate_limit.interval_seconds` must be positive if `rate_limit` is set
180: - Added `ValidationResult` interface with `valid`, `errors`, `warnings` fields
181: - Added `validateSourcesYaml()` — validates full sources.yml against `SourcesFileSchema` via safeParse, returns `ValidationResult`
182: - Added `validateSourceEntry()` — validates single source entry via safeParse, returns `ValidationResult`
183: - Both validation functions use `safeParse` (never throw)

265: - Academic/restricted journals (BMJ, Lancet, NEJM, JAMA, ICJ, ICC, OHCHR) use Google News RSS fallback pattern since their direct RSS may be paywalled
266: - Section header comments (`## HEALTH POOL`, etc.) added for file navigation

275: ## 2026-05-02: Wave 3, Task 13 — Create `src/parsers/reddit.ts`
276: 
277: ### Files Created
278: - `src/parsers/reddit.ts` — Reddit source parser
279: 
280: ### Exports
281: - `parseReddit(source: Source): Promise<NewsItem[]>` — Fetches hot posts from subreddit via `getSubredditPosts()`
282: - `parseRedditSearch(source: Source, query: string): Promise<NewsItem[]>` — Searches Reddit via `searchReddit()`
283: 
284: ### Implementation details
285: - Uses singleton `RateLimiter` instance with `REDDIT_PLATFORM_LIMIT` (60 req / 60s) registered at module level
286: - Both platform-level and per-source rate limits applied via `applyRateLimits()` helper
287: - Subreddit resolved from `source.url` (priority) or extracted from `source.id` via `extractSubredditFromId()` (`reddit_worldnews` → `worldnews`)
288: - `normalizeRedditPost()` from `reddit-api.ts` handles the RedditPost → NewsItem transformation
289: - Errors caught gracefully — returns `[]` with `console.error` log (pipeline resilience)
290: - `parseRedditSearch` scopes search to source's subreddit via `searchReddit({ subreddits: [subreddit], ... })`
291: - Uses existing `RedditPost` type, no new types introduced
292: 
293: ### Key decisions
294: - Singleton limiter (module-level `const`) ensures all Reddit sources share one platform quota
295: - `extractSubredditFromId` falls back to raw id if no `reddit_` prefix — handles edge cases gracefully
296: - `resolveSubreddit` helper centralizes the url/id priority logic used by both functions
297: - Batch size from `source.rate_limit?.batch_size || 25`; timeout 30s for platform rate-limit wait

305: ## 2026-05-02: Wave 3, Task 14 — Create `src/utils/twitter-api.ts`
306: 
307: ### Files Created
308: - `src/utils/twitter-api.ts` — Twitter/X API v2 utility with Nitter fallback
309: 
310: ### Exports
311: - `Tweet` interface — Full tweet structure matching Twitter API v2 response
312: - `TwitterClient` class with `getUserTimeline(username, maxResults?)` — Fetches recent tweets
313: - `normalizeTweet(tweet, sourceName, poolId)` — Maps Tweet → NewsItem shape
314: 
315: ### Implementation: Dual-mode strategy
316: 
317: **Mode 1: Twitter API v2 (preferred)**
318: - Reads `TWITTER_BEARER_TOKEN` from environment (never hardcoded)
319: - Resolves username → numeric user ID via `/2/users/by/username/{username}`
320: - Fetches tweets via `/2/users/{userId}/tweets` with `tweet.fields`, `user.fields`, `expansions`
321: - Enriches tweets with `author_username` and `author_name` from `includes.users`
322: - Max 100 tweets per request (API limit enforced)
323: - Rate-limited via `RateLimiter` with `TWITTER_PLATFORM_LIMIT` (450 req/15min)
324: 
325: **Mode 2: Nitter RSS scraping (fallback)**
326: - Tries multiple Nitter instances in order: nitter.net, nitter.privacydev.net, nitter.1d4.us, nitter.kavin.rocks
327: - Parses RSS feed with regex-based `parseRSSFeed()` supporting both `<item>` and `<entry>` elements
328: - Extracts tweet ID from `/status/{id}` in link
329: - Decodes HTML entities in descriptions via `decodeHtmlEntities()`
330: - Returns best-effort results with source annotation
331: 
332: ### normalizeTweet mapping:
333: - `id` → `tweet_{id}`
334: - `link` → `https://twitter.com/{username}/status/{id}`
335: - `pubDate` → from `created_at` ISO string
336: - `content_snippet` → tweet text + first expanded URL
337: - `media_url` → first URL matching image extension pattern
338: 
339: ### Key Decisions
340: - No external npm dependencies — uses `undici` (already in project) for HTTP
341: - `import { request } from 'undici'` matches pattern in `reddit-api.ts`
342: - Module-level singleton `RateLimiter` ensures all Twitter sources share one platform quota
343: - `getUserTimeline` strips `@` prefix and lowercases usernames
344: - Nitter instances tried sequentially; stops on first successful response with tweets
345: - `fetchViaAPI` returns `[]` if `TWITTER_BEARER_TOKEN` is missing, triggering Nitter fallback
346: - RSS parser supports both RSS `<item>` and Atom `<entry>` elements for broader compatibility
347: - `TWITTER_PLATFORM_LIMIT` already existed in `rate-limiter.ts` — no changes needed there
348:

372: - Checks BOTH `source.platform` (preferred) and `source.parser` (fallback) for robustness
373: - No HTTP cache layer for social media — Reddit/Twitter rate limiting IS the cache
374: - Rest of `parseBySource()` completely unchanged
375: - `filterByKeywords` needs no changes — both `normalizeRedditPost` and `normalizeTweet` populate `content_snippet`

395: - Documented 366 sources across 14 pools, 50 countries, 31 domain tags
396: - Inventory: 338 RSS, 11 HTTP, 17 social_media (10 Reddit, 7 Twitter)
397: - 42 sources have explicit tier assignments, 17 have rate_limit config
398: - Covered all required sections: schema, pools, tiers, rate limiting, pre-validation, social media config, adding new sources

402: ## Task 22: Create performance benchmark script at `scripts/performance-test.ts`
403: 
404: ### Files Created
405: - `scripts/performance-test.ts` — Standalone performance benchmark
406: 
407: ### Test Scenarios
408: 
409: 1. **Single source baseline** — Fetches 1 RSS source and 1 social_media source. RSS uses HTTP GET via undici measuring full fetch + body download latency. social_media is skipped (requires platform auth) with a note explaining why.
410: 
411: 2. **Batch throughput** — Selects 10 active RSS sources, fetches all concurrently with `Promise.allSettled`, measures total wall-clock time and per-source breakdown. Completed in 1111ms with all 10 succeeding.
412: 
413: 3. **Cache efficiency** — Fetches the same RSS source twice sequentially, reports both timings. Both fetches returned in 12ms each (Google News RSS responses are small/fast).

## 2026-05-02: Wave 4, Task 20 — Create comprehensive validation suite at `scripts/full-validation.ts`

### Files Created
- `scripts/full-validation.ts` — Comprehensive IGS configuration validation suite

### 8 Validation Checks

1. **YAML Syntax** — Parses pools.yml, sources.yml, settings.yml, countries.yml. Catches YAML parse errors (malformed files, missing files)
2. **Schema Validation** — Imports `SourcesFileSchema` and `PoolsFileSchema` from `src/config/schemas.js`, validates parsed data via `safeParse()`. Reports full Zod error details (path + message)
3. **Source IDs — No Duplicates** — Checks all source IDs for uniqueness. Found duplicate `moscow_times` at indices 291 and 309
4. **Pool References** — Collects all valid pool IDs from pools.yml (18 pools), verifies every `sources.pools[]` entry exists
5. **Required Fields** — Every source must have `id`, `name`, at least one of `url` or `platform`, and non-empty `pools` array. Also validates `type` is one of rss/http/social_media
6. **Tier Values** — All tier values must be 1, 2, or 3 if present (42/366 sources have tier assignments)
7. **Rate Limits** — Every `type: social_media` source must have `rate_limit` with `interval_seconds` positive and optional `batch_size` positive (17 social_media sources, all valid)
8. **URL Format** — Non-social_media sources (rss/http) must have parseable URLs (verifies with `new URL()`). Skips social_media

### Output Format
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-05-02T16:50:08.461Z",
  "totalChecks": 8,
  "passed": 6,
  "failed": 2,
  "checks": [
    { "name": "YAML Syntax", "passed": true, "errors": [], "warnings": [...] },
    { "name": "Schema Validation", "passed": false, "errors": [...], "warnings": [...] },
    ...
  ]
}
```

### Key Implementation Details
- Uses `js-yaml` for YAML parsing (existing dep), dynamic import from `../src/config/schemas.js` for Zod schemas
- YAML files loaded once and shared across all checks (avoids redundant I/O)
- All checks run in parallel via `Promise.all` for performance
- Human-readable summary on stderr, structured JSON on stdout (pipeline-friendly)
- Exit code 0 = all pass, 1 = any failure

### Findings
- **Schema validation fails on 17 social_media sources** — The Zod schema requires `url: z.string().url()` for ALL sources, but social_media sources use identifiers (subreddit names, twitter handles) instead of URLs. The `url` field in the schema should likely use `.url().or(z.string())` for social_media sources, or the schema needs a `superRefine` to make URL optional when `type === 'social_media'`
- **Duplicate source ID `moscow_times`** at indices 291 and 309 — two sources share the same ID
- All other checks pass cleanly (YAML syntax, pool references, required fields, tier values, rate limits, URL format)

### Verification
- Exit code: 1 (correct — 2 checks failed)
- JSON output is valid
- `bun run scripts/full-validation.ts` runs successfully
- No existing files modified

+3. **Source IDs — No Duplicates** — Checks all source IDs for uniqueness. Found duplicate `moscow_times` at indices 291 and 309
+4. **Pool References** — Collects all valid pool IDs from pools.yml (18 pools), verifies every `sources.pools[]` entry exists
+5. **Required Fields** — Every source must have `id`, `name`, at least one of `url` or `platform`, and non-empty `pools` array. Also validates `type` is one of rss/http/social_media
+6. **Tier Values** — All tier values must be 1, 2, or 3 if present (42/366 sources have tier assignments)
+7. **Rate Limits** — Every `type: social_media` source must have `rate_limit` with `interval_seconds` positive and optional `batch_size` positive (17 social_media sources, all valid)
+8. **URL Format** — Non-social_media sources (rss/http) must have parseable URLs (verifies with `new URL()`). Skips social_media
+
+### Output Format
+```json
+{
+  "timestamp": "2026-05-02T16:50:08.461Z",
+  "totalChecks": 8,
+  "passed": 6,
+  "failed": 2,
+  "checks": [
+    { "name": "YAML Syntax", "passed": true, "errors": [], "warnings": [...] },
+    { "name": "Schema Validation", "passed": false, "errors": [...], "warnings": [...] },
+    ...
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+### Key Implementation Details
+- Uses `js-yaml` for YAML parsing (existing dep), dynamic import from `../src/config/schemas.js` for Zod schemas
+- YAML files loaded once and shared across all checks (avoids redundant I/O)
+- All checks run in parallel via `Promise.all` for performance
+- Human-readable summary on stderr, structured JSON on stdout (pipeline-friendly)
+- Exit code 0 = all pass, 1 = any failure
+
+### Findings
+- **Schema validation fails on 17 social_media sources** — The Zod schema requires `url: z.string().url()` for ALL sources, but social_media sources use identifiers (subreddit names, twitter handles) instead of URLs. The `url` field in the schema should likely use `.url().or(z.string())` for social_media sources, or the schema needs a `superRefine` to make URL optional when `type === 'social_media'`
+- **Duplicate source ID `moscow_times`** at indices 291 and 309 — two sources share the same ID
+- All other checks pass cleanly (YAML syntax, pool references, required fields, tier values, rate limits, URL format)
+
+### Verification
+- Exit code: 1 (correct — 2 checks failed)
+- JSON output is valid
+- `bun run scripts/full-validation.ts` runs successfully
+- No existing files modified
